narrative.headline: 2022 Subaru Forester: windshield and visibility lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 92 complaints with NHTSA. Visibility/wiper is the most reported area at 22%, followed by forward collision avoidance (13%) and a tie between the electrical system and engine (11% each). No deaths are reported. The engine area stands out for fires (4), while vehicle speed control draws the fewest complaints but the worst outcomes by share: 3 crashes and 3 injuries.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 20 reports (22%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Inspect the windshield closely for chips or cracks.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 12 reports (13%), tied to 1 crash. Test the driver-assist features on the road to see how they behave.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 10 reports (11%), including 1 with fire. Check that the electronics and warning lights work as expected.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 10 reports (11%), but the most fires of any area at 4, plus 1 crash. The fire count makes this worth a careful inspection.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Fewest complaints at 9 reports (10%), but the worst outcomes: 3 crashes, 3 injuries, and 1 fire. Several owners describe unintended acceleration, so test acceleration and braking carefully.
